schema.name: adventure-knowledge-graph
schema.description: A stateful, directed knowledge graph for a classic gamebook-style dungeon adventure. The graph separates executable traversal rules from a narrative-semantic layer containing world truths, rumours, characters, factions, motifs, dramatic threads, knowledge definitions and node-level story affordances.

graph.title: The Dungeons Below the Rust Wind Hills
graph.subtitle: A licensed delve for fortune, glory, and likely doom
design_notes: The town licensing system is both a comic premise and a persistent game mechanic.
Multiple routes bypass formal permission, but each creates later risks.
Key items open materially different paths rather than merely granting bonuses.
The graph contains loops, shortcuts, optional encounters, delayed consequences and several endings.

content_revision.summary: Playtest-driven state fixes: the counterfeit permit purchase (r018) is gated on permit_status so it cannot be repeated, the pit beast (r074) and the Guardian of the Ninth Lock (r090-r092) can each only be dealt with once, new unopposed transit routes (r109, r110) keep the forge and the abyssal stair reachable after those encounters are settled, and the vault antechamber gains a guaranteed retreat (r111) plus disclosure on its three gated doors so it can no longer soft-lock a delver who arrived without the right keys.
content_revision.compatibility: Existing node and route IDs, topology, costs and tests are preserved. r018, r074, r090, r091 and r092 gain gating conditions; r074 sets the new pit_beast_dead flag; r080-r082 gain disclosure; routes r109, r110 and r111 are added.
major_additions: Player-facing revelation text for all knowledge facts
Narrative resolution records for all routes
State-aware read-aloud variants at key continuity points
Mandatory exposition at the Warden and treasure vault
A narrative delivery plan connecting the adventure's acts

graph.narrative_premise: Barrowgate treats the dungeon beneath the Rust Wind Hills as a regulated natural resource. The joke is that the bureaucracy works: the old dungeon recognises permits, seals, debts and claims of office because it was never merely a tomb. It was the Ninth Treasury of a fallen kingdom, built to bind a crown that turns promises into law.
graph.core_mystery: Why do Barrowgate's crude licences interact with mechanisms three centuries older than the town, and what exactly is being licensed: entry to the dungeon, or succession to its buried authority?
graph.dramatic_question: Can the delver take the Crown of Ember Salt without becoming another official in the machinery that has outlived every kingdom and every excuse for its existence?
graph.hidden_truth: The modern licence seal descends from fragments of the old treasury seal. Each issued licence creates a faint legal identity inside the dungeon's oath-bound systems. Barrowgate believes it is charging for access; in practice it is unknowingly maintaining a broken succession ritual.

opening_context.player_facing_introduction: Rumours of a mythic treasure have drawn you to Barrowgate, a hard little town at the foot of the Rust Wind Hills. Beneath the hills lies an ancient dungeon complex that has swallowed generations of treasure-seekers. Barrowgate has turned that danger into a local industry: delvers buy licences, supplies, hired help and blessings before taking the road into the hills. Some return rich. Most return poorer. Many do not return at all.
premise_facts: A mythic treasure is believed to lie in the deepest vault.
The dungeon predates Barrowgate by centuries.
Barrowgate licences and charges delvers for access.
A licence may affect how the ancient dungeon recognises a claimant.
The player begins in town and chooses how much to prepare before entering the hills.
opening_context.narrative_function: Orient the player before the first choices without revealing the hidden succession machinery.

condition_dsl.combination: All conditions on a route must pass unless the route uses an explicit any block.

test_types.skill: Roll 2d6; succeed if total is less than or equal to the named stat after modifiers.
test_types.luck: Roll 2d6; succeed if total is less than or equal to current luck, then reduce luck by 1.
test_types.combat: Resolve repeated attack rounds using player skill/stamina against encounter values.
test_types.choice: No roll; outcome is determined by state, cost or declared action.

narrative.surface_read: A rough frontier town with a comic quantity of forms, fees and warnings.
narrative.deeper_truth: Barrowgate has built an economy around managed danger and unknowingly inherited fragments of royal authority.
narrative.dramatic_function: Establish the licensing joke, make preparation meaningful and seed every major mystery.
questions_raised: Why does a town permit matter to an ancient dungeon?
What kind of person accepts risk after signing it?

narrative.surface_read: Exposed hills containing ruins, rival routes and petty dangers.
narrative.deeper_truth: The landscape preserves competing memories of state power, smuggling and rebellion.
narrative.dramatic_function: Let the player choose what kind of claimant they will be before the dungeon begins judging them.
questions_raised: Is the official entrance safer, or merely more legible?
When is bypassing authority an act of sense rather than guilt?

narrative.surface_read: A dead garrison inhabited by vermin, mechanisms and an undead inspector.
narrative.deeper_truth: The upper levels were designed to convert bodies into recognised legal identities before allowing deeper access.
narrative.dramatic_function: Show that the bureaucracy is not decoration; it is the dungeon's operating system.
questions_raised: Can a correct process produce an unjust result?
Are the undead enforcing law, or hiding inside it?

regions.name: Mid Dungeon
regions.theme: forgotten civic machinery and predatory ecology

narrative.surface_read: A varied sequence of hazards, puzzles, archives and predatory spaces.
narrative.deeper_truth: These rooms reveal how the old state recorded, extracted and enforced obligation.
narrative.dramatic_function: Turn the delver from trespasser into investigator and give key items narrative meaning.
questions_raised: What was the treasury protecting people from—and what was it doing to them?
Which burdens are worth carrying into the deep?

regions.name: Lower Dungeon / Deep Ruins
regions.theme: debts, dead kingdoms and sealed knowledge

narrative.surface_read: An underworld of debts, dead traders, drowned law and custodial monsters.
narrative.deeper_truth: Identity itself has become a negotiable resource because the Crown monopolised recognition.
narrative.dramatic_function: Make lore mechanically actionable and expose alternatives to simple conquest.
questions_raised: What remains of a person after memory, name and duty separate?
Is release more merciful than victory?

narrative.surface_read: A final gauntlet, judgement and treasure chamber.
narrative.deeper_truth: The final region is a succession trial disguised as a dungeon climax.
narrative.dramatic_function: Force the player to define what taking the treasure means.
sensory_palette: the smell of rain on stone
questions_raised: Does reaching power create a right to use it?
Can someone accept office without becoming its previous occupant?

items.name: Standard Delver Licence
items.description: A wax-sealed licence permitting one named delver to enter the public approaches to the dungeon.

narrative_semantics.origin: Issued by Barrowgate using a seal cut from old treasury brass.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Legitimacy manufactured through procedure, yet made unexpectedly real.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: The modern town has inherited a fragment of ancient authority.

items.name: Counterfeit Permit
items.description: A convincing forgery unless checked against the iron registry.

narrative_semantics.origin: Forged by the Red Quill using a rebel flaw copied from erased records.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: A false document that may express a truer claim than the official one.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: Recognition and truth are separate systems.

items.name: Delver's Supplies
items.description: Rope, chalk, iron spikes, lamp oil and three things the merchant insists are medicinal.

narrative_semantics.origin: Assembled from practical goods and equipment recovered from previous delves.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Preparation, inheritance and the material residue of other people's failures.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: Adventure in Barrowgate is an economy before it is a legend.

items.name: Torchbearer Contract
items.description: A contract with Pell Vane. Pell carries light, can secure one dangerous crossing, and refuses to pass beyond the Ninth Lock.

narrative_semantics.origin: A standard yard contract amended by Pell in his own hand.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: The boundary between treating a companion as equipment and recognising their agency.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: Some of the dungeon's most meaningful limits are human rather than magical.

items.name: Token of the Dust Saint
items.description: A little clay eye. Warm when hidden doors are nearby.

narrative_semantics.origin: A clay eye based on symbols used by Ilya's escape network.

items.name: Goblin Toll Chit
items.description: A stamped scrap proving the bearer has paid one goblin toll, probably.

narrative_semantics.origin: Stamped by the Rust Wind Toll Union using a deliberately impressive quantity of ink.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Authority emerging from repeated social performance.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: The dungeon's logic of recognition is spreading through imitation.

items.name: Brass Seal
items.description: A heavy seal engraved with the emblem of the Ninth Treasury.

narrative_semantics.origin: The quartermaster's authority seal for the Ninth Treasury.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Office as portable power and inherited obligation.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: The dungeon responds to recognised roles, not merely keys.

items.name: Moon Key
items.description: A crescent key that only appears solid in reflected light.

narrative_semantics.origin: A key removed from the official record and preserved only as reflection.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Access belonging to people history refused to acknowledge.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: Erasure cannot wholly destroy what other systems still remember.

items.name: Stolen Ledger Fragment
items.description: A page recording debts owed by dead nobles to the custodians of the vault.

narrative_semantics.origin: A page torn from the treasury accounts during the Salt Revolt.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: The guardians' obligations can be interpreted, completed and released.

items.name: Rune-Candle
items.description: A black candle whose flame reveals inscriptions, invisible contracts and certain lies.

narrative_semantics.origin: Forged from black wax used to reveal text hidden beneath official seals.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Truth as illumination that creates responsibility rather than comfort.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: Many objects and offices carry concealed language.

items.name: Registrar's Key
items.description: A severe iron key carried by the dungeon's undead licensing official.

narrative_semantics.origin: Halvek's office key, shaped for both prison locks and record cabinets.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Violently acquired authority that remains institutionally recognisable.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: Systems often recognise possession before justice.

items.name: The Crown of Ember Salt
items.description: A crown of red crystal and gold that remembers every ruler who wore it.

narrative_semantics.origin: Forged for the first sovereign of Veyr and enlarged by every recognised reign.
narrative_semantics.symbolic_role: Power as accumulated promise, memory and loss of personal boundary.
narrative_semantics.world_revelation: The Crown is the source and culmination of the dungeon's logic.

encounters.name: Goblin Toll Collectors
encounters.special: They accept coins, official-looking paperwork or sufficiently confident nonsense.

narrative_semantics.desire: Be treated as a legitimate institution because legitimacy is safer than banditry.
narrative_semantics.fear: That travellers will notice the Union has no authority beyond collective confidence.
narrative_semantics.misconception: They believe human administration is powerful because it is complicated.
narrative_semantics.voice: Cheerfully procedural, argumentative about exemptions and proud of receipts.
narrative_semantics.aftermath: Whether paid, bluffed or fought, the encounter establishes that paperwork shapes behaviour above the dungeon too.

encounters.name: The Rat-King of Broken Spears
encounters.special: At half stamina, the swarm extinguishes one torch turn per combat round.

narrative_semantics.desire: Retain the keys and the identity of Quartermaster Orrick.
narrative_semantics.fear: Dissolving into ordinary rats and unowned memories.
narrative_semantics.misconception: The swarm believes possession of Orrick's inventory proves it is Orrick.
non_combat_leverage: a promise to preserve the name
narrative_semantics.aftermath: Death clears the route but destroys a strange surviving personhood.

encounters.name: The Iron Registrar
encounters.special: Counterfeit documents impose a penalty unless the registry has been sabotaged.

narrative_semantics.desire: Complete a lawful inspection and receive a discharge it cannot request.
narrative_semantics.fear: Using discretion and repeating the moral failure of its living office.
narrative_semantics.misconception: Halvek treats procedural neutrality as innocence.
narrative_semantics.voice: Exact, metallic and incapable of casual phrasing.
narrative_semantics.aftermath: Peaceful passage reveals the system's strange legitimacy; violence creates an adverse record that persists.

encounters.name: The Cistern Eels
encounters.special: Metal equipment attracts the largest eel.

narrative_semantics.desire: Feed on oath residue and electrical disturbance.
narrative_semantics.fear: Open flame and the stillness following a fully admitted truth.
narrative_semantics.misconception: Not applicable; they are animals altered by the dungeon's residue.
narrative_semantics.voice: No speech; react to lies with sudden blue light.
narrative_semantics.aftermath: The encounter makes truth physically consequential before the final succession trial.

encounters.name: The Beast of the Pits
encounters.special: It fears open flame but becomes stronger if wounded by forged iron.

narrative_semantics.desire: Escape the role of spectacle and property.
narrative_semantics.fear: Crowd noise, forged iron and handlers' bells.
narrative_semantics.misconception: It associates stamped authority with pain.
narrative_semantics.voice: No speech; behaviour changes sharply around seals and applause.
narrative_semantics.aftermath: Victory by simple killing risks repeating the old state's claim that control proves ownership.

encounters.name: Guardian of the Ninth Lock
encounters.special: It can be defeated, formally released, or compelled by proving an ancient debt.

narrative_semantics.desire: Have its debt named and completed.
narrative_semantics.fear: Destruction before lawful release, which would make centuries of duty meaningless.
narrative_semantics.misconception: Varn assumes only formal authority can recognise personhood.
narrative_semantics.voice: Slow, ceremonial and structured as legal recital.
narrative_semantics.aftermath: The chosen resolution defines whether the player sees guardians as monsters, tools or persons.

encounters.name: The Warden Below
encounters.special: The Warden recognises lawful authority, ancient debts and the voice of the Crown.

narrative_semantics.desire: Recognise a successor fit to face the Crown and end the vacancy.
narrative_semantics.fear: A conqueror whose appetite will be amplified into law.
narrative_semantics.misconception: Serath may overvalue restraint that resembles old custodial obedience.
narrative_semantics.aftermath: The Warden's judgement reframes the whole dungeon as a succession test.

nodes.title: Barrowgate
nodes.summary: A hard frontier town that has built a living around licensed expeditions into the ancient dungeons beneath the Rust Wind Hills.
nodes.read_aloud: Rumours of a mythic treasure have drawn you to Barrowgate, a hard little town at the foot of the Rust Wind Hills. Beneath those iron-red slopes, ancient dungeons have swallowed generations of delvers. The town has made a business of the danger: licences, supplies, hired torchbearers, blessings and, when needed, memorial plaques. Rust-red dust moves along the street like smoke, carrying the faint iron smell of the hills. Above the gatehouse hangs a board: DELVERS MUST BE LICENSED. NO REFUNDS.

narrative.arrival_text: The player arrives because rumours of a mythic treasure have spread beyond the hills. Barrowgate immediately presents the dungeon as both mortal danger and ordinary local business.
narrative.local_history: The square was once a livestock market. The first dungeon licence was issued here after three rival parties killed one another over salvage rights.
narrative.present_tension: The town depends upon delvers while knowing that many of them will not return.
narrative.hidden_truth: Several memorial plaques are made from metal recovered from the old treasury seal.
sensory_details: red dust collecting in boot seams
narrative_hooks: Make the dungeon, the treasure rumour and the licensing industry explicit before presenting choices.
Show that licences, equipment, hired help and blessings are normal parts of preparing a delve.
Keep the deeper reason the dungeon recognises paperwork hidden at this stage.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Public confidence stretched over private unease.
sensory_focus: a gong note hanging in the square

nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Barrowgate's square is still busy with the trade of dungeon-going. Red grit rasps underfoot, and the iron smell of the hills rides the wind between the licensing hall, market and inn.

nodes.title: The Bent Nail Inn
nodes.summary: The principal market for rumours, boastful survivors and people selling maps they definitely drew themselves.

narrative.arrival_text: Every table has been carved with a map. No two maps agree, including those carved by the same hand.
narrative.local_history: The inn took its name from the first licensed delver, who returned with one bent nail and no companions.
narrative.present_tension: Rumour is both entertainment and informal risk assessment.
narrative.hidden_truth: The proprietor quietly compares new stories against a private list of details only genuine survivors mention.
narrative_hooks: Rumours should contradict in detail but converge in motif.
A survivor refuses to say the Warden's name.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Curiosity tempered by the habit of watching strangers fail.

nodes.read_aloud: Warm, sour air rolls from the Bent Nail Inn. Low voices gather beneath the smoke-black beams, then thin as you enter. A scarred delver turns his cup between both hands. "The treasure is a crown," he says, "and the deep places listen when you make promises." A torchbearer at the next table adds, without looking up, "No hired hand crosses the Ninth Lock twice." Before you leave, the innkeeper mentions two quieter leads: a red feather painted beneath certain windows, and a blind roadside saint whose shrines mark paths the council never licensed.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The Bent Nail closes around you in smoke and low conversation. The same warnings are being told to someone newer.

rumour_delivery.speaker: A scarred delver
rumour_delivery.text: The treasure is a crown, and the deep places listen when you make promises.

rumour_delivery.speaker: A hired torchbearer
rumour_delivery.text: No hired hand crosses the Ninth Lock twice.

rumour_delivery.speaker: The innkeeper
rumour_delivery.text: A red feather beneath a window marks someone who can make the council believe almost anything.

rumour_delivery.speaker: The innkeeper
rumour_delivery.text: The blind saint keeps one road hidden from officials and another hidden from the lost.

nodes.title: Market of Last Chances
nodes.summary: A row of stalls selling rope, spikes, charms, questionable tonics and second-hand armour with suspicious dents.

narrative.arrival_text: Merchants call out guarantees that become narrower the closer anyone reads the chalkboard.
narrative.local_history: The market grew around the practice of reselling equipment recovered from bodies whose families could not pay collection fees.
narrative.present_tension: Every useful object carries an implied previous failure.
narrative.hidden_truth: One stallholder recognises old treasury markings but pretends they are decorative.
narrative_hooks: Describe one item with a former owner's initials.
Let bargains feel practical rather than magical.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Commercial briskness with grief kept carefully out of sight.

nodes.read_aloud: Oil, old leather and sharp medicinal vinegar mingle between the stalls. Merchants call out cheerful guarantees, but several keep glancing at the road to the hills as though expecting their stock to return without its owner.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Buckles click, oil lamps hiss and a merchant quietly turns a dented helm so its worst damage faces the wall.

nodes.title: Torchbearers' Yard
nodes.summary: Labourers wait beneath a sign reading: HALF PAY IN ADVANCE. FULL PAY IF WE RETURN.

narrative.arrival_text: The waiting workers study delvers the way sailors study weather.
narrative.local_history: The yard began as a widows' mutual fund and slowly became a hiring exchange.
narrative.present_tension: Delvers think they are choosing assistants; the assistants are deciding which delvers are survivable.
narrative.hidden_truth: Pell has refused three famous expeditions and been proved right each time.
narrative_hooks: Pell asks what the player will do when someone weaker says stop.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Pell: 'I carry light. I don't carry decisions.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Caution disguised as professional appraisal.

nodes.read_aloud: The yard smells of tarred rope, lamp smoke and damp wool. The waiting torchbearers look you over in silence. One man rubs warmth into his fingers before offering his contract.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Lamp flames tremble in the draught. The workers' attention settles on you again, practical and unsentimental.

nodes.title: Temple of the Last Cinder
nodes.summary: A narrow shrine where delvers may purchase absolution before creating the need for it.

narrative.arrival_text: A priest asks for the delver's true name, then writes it in ash where no ledger can keep it.
narrative.local_history: The temple was founded by survivors who believed the old kingdom's records could trap the dead.
narrative.present_tension: The rite offers comfort while implying that identity itself may be at risk.
narrative.hidden_truth: The clergy know the Crown stores personalities but cannot prove whether blessing resists it.
narrative_hooks: A priest warns against making promises underground.
The ash shifts when the Crown is named.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Kindness shaped by long familiarity with fear.

nodes.read_aloud: The temple is warmer than the street, scented with beeswax and singed rosemary. A priest waits beside a shallow bowl of ash, their voice gentle but their hands very steady.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Warm ash and beeswax soften the air. The priest remembers you and does not ask why you have returned.

nodes.title: The Licensing Hall
nodes.summary: A civic hall whose clerks treat dungeon access as a revenue stream with unfortunate mortality statistics.

narrative.arrival_text: Applicants queue beneath a mural of a heroic delver shaking hands with a clerk. The delver's painted face has been retouched many times.
narrative.local_history: The hall was built over the workshop where the first modern seal was cut from quarry brass.
narrative.present_tension: The process genuinely reduces danger, which makes its fees and exclusions harder to dismiss.
narrative.hidden_truth: Ollis Marr's signatures carry a trace of authority the old dungeon may interpret as delegated office.
narrative_hooks: Forms list absurd hazards beside disturbingly specific ones.
A seal briefly warms in the applicant's hand.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Ollis: 'The dungeon is not obliged to recognise you. This document makes the attempt.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Weariness, responsibility and a trace of doubt beneath practiced formality.

nodes.read_aloud: Hot sealing wax and dry paper give the hall its close, dusty smell. Pens scratch behind the counters. A clerk smooths the same corner of your form twice before meeting your eye.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The pens continue scratching. Somewhere behind the counter, a stamp falls with the weight of a small verdict.

nodes.title: The Red Quill Cellar
nodes.summary: A concealed workshop below a cobbler's shop. Its proprietor forges permits but refuses responsibility for iron registrars.

narrative.arrival_text: Names cover the walls in red ink: people absent from every official census but remembered here.
narrative.local_history: The Red Quill began as an archive of families erased during the Salt Revolt.
narrative.present_tension: Maera can create a document the dungeon may accept, but she refuses to call that the same thing as legitimacy.
narrative.hidden_truth: Her counterfeit seal includes an intentional flaw matching a rebel alteration found in the Sunken Archive.
narrative_hooks: Maera asks whether the player wants access or recognition.
One erased surname matches a modern Barrowgate family.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Maera: 'Official does not mean true. Counterfeit does not mean false.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Guarded interest sharpened by old anger.

nodes.read_aloud: The cellar air is damp and metallic with iron-gall ink. Red names cover the walls. Maera studies you over a cooling seal, one eyebrow lifted—not amused, exactly, but interested.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The ink smell catches at the back of your throat. Maera's gaze flicks first to your hands, then to your face.

nodes.title: Road to the Rust Wind Hills
nodes.summary: The last maintained road before the land becomes red stone, thorn scrub and old warnings.

narrative.arrival_text: The maintained road ends at a sign giving five legal warnings and no useful directions.
narrative.local_history: Convict crews once paved the road to move ember-salt from the hills.
narrative.present_tension: Leaving town turns administrative risk into physical exposure.
narrative.hidden_truth: The road's milestone numbers correspond to treasury departments rather than distance.
narrative_hooks: The final milestone reads SUCCESSION.
narrative.emotional_undertone: The first clean edge of commitment.

nodes.read_aloud: The last maintained stones give way to red earth. Wind moves down from the hills with an iron smell, cool enough to dry the lips. Behind you, Barrowgate's roofs look suddenly small.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The wind still tastes faintly of metal. The town remains visible, but no longer feels near.

nodes.title: Wind-Carved Cairns
nodes.summary: Ancient cairns sing in the gale. One stone bears a half-erased treasury sigil.

narrative.arrival_text: The cairns hum at different pitches as the wind moves through them, like a choir trying to remember one chord.
narrative.local_history: They mark miners who died before the kingdom began recording common names.
narrative.present_tension: The stones offer the first evidence that the hills remember people differently from official history.
narrative.hidden_truth: One cairn contains ember-salt dust that responds to spoken promises.
narrative_hooks: A careless promise makes one cairn answer.
A treasury sigil has been deliberately chipped away.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Wonder made uneasy by the sense of being answered.

nodes.read_aloud: Wind threads through the cairns and draws a low, uneven note from the stones. Red crystal grit clicks against your boots, and the air tastes faintly of old coins.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The cairns hum as before, though one note now seems to follow your breathing.

nodes.title: Goblin Toll Path
nodes.summary: A rope barrier, a dented bell and six goblins who have independently invented infrastructure finance.

narrative.arrival_text: A striped barrier blocks the path. A goblin in a clerk's visor asks whether the traveller requires a receipt.
narrative.local_history: The toll began as extortion but became safer after the goblins adopted schedules, exemptions and complaint procedures.
narrative.present_tension: The scene is funny until it becomes clear that the goblins understand performed legitimacy better than most humans.
narrative.hidden_truth: Their oldest stamp was copied from a fragment found near the quarry.
narrative_hooks: A goblin cites a precedent invented moments ago.
The receipt may be more useful than expected.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Toll clerk: 'Violence is free. Crossing is two gold.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Pride and insecurity wrapped in officious confidence.

nodes.read_aloud: A painted barrier knocks gently in the wind. Behind it, onion stew simmers over a smoky fire. The goblin clerk straightens his visor when he sees you and tries not to look pleased at having a customer.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The bell gives a thin clang. The clerk reaches for his stamp before deciding whether you count as new business.

nodes.title: Collapsed Watchtower
nodes.summary: A fallen imperial watchtower overlooking all three known dungeon approaches.

narrative.arrival_text: The tower has fallen outward, as though something inside expanded after the roof collapsed.
narrative.local_history: It once watched prisoner labour and later served smugglers mapping forgotten entrances.
narrative.present_tension: It offers strategic clarity at the cost of revealing the scale of the ruin.
narrative.hidden_truth: A scratched survey mark identifies the flooded route as an archive drain.
narrative_hooks: From here the three entrances look like three moral arguments.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Clarity accompanied by the loneliness of exposure.

nodes.read_aloud: The tower stones are warm where the sun reaches them and slick with old damp below. Wind moans through the split masonry; from here, the three entrances lie exposed across the red hills.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The broken tower gives the wind a hollow voice. Dust stings your eyes as you look down over the approaches.

nodes.title: Old Quarry Entrance
nodes.summary: A broad, obvious descent used by licensed parties, tax inspectors and ambushers.

narrative.arrival_text: Fresh municipal paint surrounds an opening older than the town by centuries.
narrative.local_history: Barrowgate widened the quarry and accidentally reopened the treasury's ceremonial approach.
narrative.present_tension: This route is easiest to find and easiest for every rival, official and predator to anticipate.
narrative.hidden_truth: The public stair was designed for petitioners, not soldiers.
narrative_hooks: A notice lists opening hours for a dungeon that never closes.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Official reassurance failing at the threshold.

nodes.read_aloud: Fresh municipal paint frames stone cut centuries ago. Cold air breathes from the opening, smelling of wet iron. The wooden handrail is clammy beneath your palm.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The entrance exhales the same wet chill. The handrail has not warmed since you last touched it.

nodes.title: Smugglers' Sinkhole
nodes.summary: A cramped shaft concealed beneath thorn mats and a carved sign reading NOTHING HERE.

narrative.arrival_text: Thorn mats conceal a shaft whose handholds have been polished by generations of illicit use.
narrative.local_history: Ilya's followers cut the first bypass; later smugglers widened it and erased most rebel marks.
narrative.present_tension: The route offers freedom from inspection but no protection from what inspection once kept out.
narrative.hidden_truth: A saint's eye symbol survives beneath newer smuggler scratches.
narrative_hooks: The hidden road carries traces of both rescue and contraband.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Illicit relief mixed with the knowledge that others fled this way.

nodes.read_aloud: Crushed thyme releases under your boots as the thorn mat is pulled aside. The shaft below smells of soil and old rope; its first handhold is cold, smooth and worn by many hurried palms.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The thyme has faded, but the shaft still breathes cool earth against your face.

nodes.title: Shrine of the Dust Saint
nodes.summary: A weathered roadside shrine to the patron of hidden roads, lost names and inconvenient truths.

narrative.arrival_text: The saint's face has one open eye and one shallow cavity large enough for a coin.
narrative.local_history: The shrine commemorates Ilya's escape routes while disguising their political purpose.
narrative.present_tension: Prayer, payment and code are difficult to separate here.
narrative.hidden_truth: The coin slot operates a concealed counterweight rather than a collection box.
narrative_hooks: The shrine rewards understanding the symbol, not devotion alone.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Tenderness preserved beneath neglect.

nodes.read_aloud: The clay shrine is warm from the sun. Dried flowers whisper against its base, and fine dust has gathered in the saint's blind eye. The place feels neglected rather than abandoned.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Dust shifts softly around the shrine. The hollow eye seems darker than the stone around it.

nodes.title: The Main Stair
nodes.summary: A monumental stair descending to the Gate of Tithes. Every seventh step is engraved with a fee.

narrative.arrival_text: Each seventh step bears a fee category: petition, appeal, inheritance, confession, succession.
narrative.local_history: The stair once processed those seeking judgement from the treasury court.
narrative.present_tension: The descent frames the delver as an applicant entering a system rather than a hero entering a lair.
narrative.hidden_truth: The lowest fees are worn smooth; the word SUCCESSION remains sharp.
narrative_hooks: Counted steps create the sense of entering a procedure.
narrative.emotional_undertone: A measured descent into something that has already classified you.

nodes.read_aloud: Your steps return from below a heartbeat late. The stone rail is wet-cold to the touch, and each seventh stair bears a fee cut deep enough to catch your boot.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The stair repeats your footfall down into darkness. Moisture beads on the rail like sweat.

nodes.title: The Hidden Shaft
nodes.summary: A narrow vertical route descending behind the upper garrison rooms.

narrative.arrival_text: The shaft avoids every ceremonial chamber and opens behind the places officials once stood.
narrative.local_history: Rebels, servants and frightened clerks used it to move people outside recognised routes.
narrative.present_tension: It feels liberating but also intimate, full of evidence left by those fleeing.
narrative.hidden_truth: Names scratched into the wall correspond to families absent from the old census.
narrative_hooks: Read fragments of names during descent.
One name also appears in the Red Quill cellar.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Claustrophobia softened by evidence that others survived the same passage.

nodes.read_aloud: The shaft presses close around your shoulders. Rope fibres rasp against your palms, and somewhere below, claws tick briefly over stone before falling silent.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The narrow walls hold your breath close. Old scratches pass beneath your fingers in the dark.

nodes.title: The Flooded Tunnel
nodes.summary: A submerged service tunnel carrying black water into the dungeon cisterns.

narrative.arrival_text: Black water presses against a low ceiling marked with archival shelf numbers.
narrative.local_history: The tunnel drained the Sunken Archive before deliberate flooding sealed it.
narrative.present_tension: The route bypasses authority by entering through the failure of old infrastructure.
narrative.hidden_truth: Scraps of parchment in the water contain names that remain legible when everything else dissolves.
narrative_hooks: Let the water whisper a name the character has not given.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Physical reluctance overridden by necessity.

nodes.read_aloud: Black water closes over your boots with a cold that bites straight through leather. It smells of stone and metal, and a faint electric taste gathers on your tongue.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The water finds the same seams in your clothing. Blue light trembles once beneath the surface.

nodes.title: Gate of Tithes
nodes.summary: An iron gate guarded by mechanisms that recognise seals, coins and blood in that order.

narrative.arrival_text: Slots in the iron gate wait for coin, seal or blood. The order is not decorative.
narrative.local_history: The gate converted visitors into recognised categories before admitting them to the treasury.
narrative.present_tension: It makes paperwork mechanically real for the first time.
narrative.hidden_truth: Blood is accepted only as collateral, not identity; the gate remembers unpaid passage.
narrative_hooks: The gate reacts differently to licence and counterfeit.
A voice asks what office the delver claims.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Impersonal judgement felt as intimate threat.

nodes.read_aloud: The iron gate stands beaded with cold moisture. Three slots wait at chest height: coin, seal and blood. Behind the bars, clockwork turns with the slow sound of something breathing in sleep.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The gate's iron is slick beneath your fingers. The hidden mechanism continues its patient breathing.

read_aloud_variants.text: The licence warms as you approach. One of the gate's mechanisms turns towards it with a soft, almost deferential click.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Show that Barrowgate's licence has genuine ancient recognition.

read_aloud_variants.text: The counterfeit permit grows warm, then cools. The gate's mechanism hesitates as though comparing two imperfect memories.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Signal that the forgery may work but is not fully trusted.

read_aloud_variants.text: No seal answers the gate. The coin and blood slots remain open while the mechanism waits for you to define yourself another way.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Clarify the practical consequence of entering without paperwork.

nodes.title: Hall of Broken Spears
nodes.summary: A barracks hall where snapped weapons have been planted upright like a dead forest.

narrative.arrival_text: Hundreds of snapped spears stand upright in the floor, each bearing a tiny stamped number.
narrative.local_history: Weapons surrendered by petitioners were catalogued here and later broken during the revolt.
narrative.present_tension: The hall makes military defeat look like administrative inventory.
narrative.hidden_truth: Some spear numbers match case files in the Taxman's Ledger Room.
narrative_hooks: A spear carries a family name preserved by the Red Quill.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Military ruin made unnervingly orderly.

nodes.read_aloud: Broken spearheads rise from the floor in ordered rows. A draught moves among them, making the shafts tap together down the long hall. The sound travels farther than it should.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Wood taps softly against wood, the echoes passing from row to row as though a message is being relayed.

nodes.title: Rat-King Warren
nodes.summary: A nest built around the old quartermaster's throne. The Rat-King wears twelve keys and none fit normal locks.

narrative.arrival_text: Keys hang from roots and ribs. The swarm speaks before any single rat becomes visible.
narrative.local_history: Quartermaster Orrick died here protecting stores; ember-salt dust preserved fragments of his final inventory across the rats that fed on him.
narrative.present_tension: The encounter can be read as monster, office-holder or terrified memory refusing dissolution.
narrative.hidden_truth: Orrick no longer knows whether the keys are possessions, duties or pieces of his name.
sensory_details: many small breaths becoming one voice
narrative_hooks: Use formal quartermaster language amid animal movement.
After death, ordinary rats avoid the little crown.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Possessiveness born from terror of dissolution.
sensory_focus: keys chiming in many small movements

nodes.read_aloud: The air is warm with grain dust and animal musk. Keys chime in the dark, first singly, then in a hundred small movements. When the voice speaks, its formality cannot quite hide its fear of being forgotten.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Brass keys stir among the roots. The warren is quieter now, but not empty.

read_aloud_variants.text: The warren is quieter now. Ordinary rats scatter from the little crown, and the keys hang without the voice that once counted them.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Prevent the Rat-King from feeling resurrected after death.

nodes.title: The Chained Well
nodes.summary: A dry well sealed by chains bearing treasury seals. Something below repeatedly asks for the correct form.

narrative.arrival_text: Chains descend into a dry well. From below, a patient voice asks for a form that no longer exists.
narrative.local_history: The well was a secure lift for sealed documents and confiscated objects.
narrative.present_tension: The puzzle turns the Brass Seal from loot into recognised authority.
narrative.hidden_truth: The voice belongs to an archive mechanism still waiting to receive the final inventory.
narrative_hooks: The mechanism thanks the player by an obsolete title.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Absurdity edged by loneliness.

nodes.read_aloud: The chains are cold enough to numb your fingers. They vanish into a dry shaft that smells of paper and deep stone. A voice rises from below, patient and faintly hopeful, asking for the correct form.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The well returns your breathing in a thin echo. The voice below waits without complaint.

nodes.title: The Mapmaker's Bones
nodes.summary: A dead surveyor lies clutching a map drawn on the inside of his own shield.

narrative.arrival_text: A skeleton hugs a shield whose inner surface carries a map corrected until metal shows through.
narrative.local_history: Surveyor Nema Tal tried to map spaces that rearranged according to legal status.
narrative.present_tension: The map proves that topology and recognition are entangled.
narrative.hidden_truth: Several impossible lines become possible only when the bearer possesses the right item or knowledge.
narrative_hooks: The shield map should change meaning as the player learns more.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Frustrated determination lingering after death.

nodes.read_aloud: Bone dust shifts beneath your knee as you turn the dead surveyor's shield. The scratched lines catch your fingernail, crossing and recrossing where no fixed corridor should.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The metal map feels colder than the room. Some lines seem deeper than you remember.

nodes.title: Chapel of Ash
nodes.summary: A roofless chapel where old vows remain audible as whispers beneath the ash.

narrative.arrival_text: Old vows repeat underfoot whenever ash is disturbed.
narrative.local_history: Officials once confessed conflicts of interest here before entering deeper offices.
narrative.present_tension: The chapel shows that the old system understood corruption but answered it with more procedure.
narrative.hidden_truth: Ilya hid names beneath the altar so the dead could be remembered outside treasury records.
narrative_hooks: A vow spoken here may echo later at the Crown.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Remorse preserved as ritual.

nodes.read_aloud: Ash lies soft across the floor and gives beneath each step. Disturbed vows whisper from underfoot, too quiet to follow and too human to dismiss. The air holds a trace of old incense.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Your footprints remain in the ash. Somewhere beneath them, a voice resumes a sentence.

nodes.title: The Iron Registrar
nodes.summary: An armoured undead official who has continued inspecting permits for three hundred and eleven years.

narrative.arrival_text: The armoured figure raises one hand. Every loose paper in the room aligns itself.
narrative.local_history: Halvek processed claims during the Salt Revolt and used procedure to avoid seeing what those claims enabled.
narrative.present_tension: The Registrar is obstacle, witness and prisoner of its own refusal to exercise judgement.
narrative.hidden_truth: It wants discharge but cannot ask for mercy without treating mercy as a valid category.
sensory_details: a voice through a closed visor
narrative_hooks: Let correct paperwork expose moral wrongness rather than solve it cleanly.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Halvek: 'Your document is legible. Your purpose remains improperly declared.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Guilt compressed into procedural control.

nodes.read_aloud: The armoured figure raises one hand, and every loose page in the room settles into line. Hot iron and dry ink sharpen the air. Behind the visor, the pause before it speaks feels almost like dread.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Paper edges align with a dry whisper. The Registrar's gauntlet remains raised, very still.

read_aloud_variants.text: Before you speak, a red strip of paper unrolls from the Registrar's desk. Your description is already written upon it. Halvek's posture becomes even more rigid.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Make the adverse record visible rather than purely mechanical.

nodes.title: The Delvers' Gaol
nodes.summary: Cells for trespassers, fraudulent applicants and parties unable to produce itemised expenses.

narrative.arrival_text: Names, appeals and release dates cover the walls. Many release dates are centuries in the future.
narrative.local_history: The cells held debtors and unrecognised persons whose cases could not be resolved without acknowledging them.
narrative.present_tension: Administrative detention becomes literal architecture.
narrative.hidden_truth: One cell contains records proving that legal erasure was used to make prisoners impossible to release.
narrative_hooks: An appeal form asks for a registry number the prisoner was denied.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Helplessness prolonged by courteous process.
sensory_focus: pages turning through a long echo

nodes.read_aloud: The cell door handle is wet-cold and leaves rust on your palm. Somewhere down the corridor, a page turns, then another; each sound returns in a longer, emptier echo.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Rust stains your hand again. The corridor gives back every small movement as though someone else is making it.

nodes.title: Fungal Cloister
nodes.summary: Monastic arches choked with pale fungus that mimics whispered prayers.

narrative.arrival_text: Pale caps bow along the cloister as if continuing a service after the monks are gone.
narrative.local_history: Monks cultivated memory-eating fungus to help officials forget confidential testimony.
narrative.present_tension: The hazard makes memory loss atmospheric before it becomes currency in the Ossuary Market.
narrative.hidden_truth: The spores preserve discarded memories and may speak them through new hosts.
narrative_hooks: Use memories belonging to erased citizens.
One fungal prayer names Ilya as a traitor and saviour.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Familiarity becoming quietly invasive.

nodes.read_aloud: The cloister smells sweet at first, then faintly rotten. Pale caps bow along the arches, and their soft whispering carries the intimacy of people speaking in the next room.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: White dust settles on your sleeves. The whispered prayers now contain a phrase you almost remember.

nodes.title: Mirror Maze
nodes.summary: Silvered passages reflect possible choices, including several the delver has not yet made.

narrative.arrival_text: Reflections continue walking when the delver stops.
narrative.local_history: The maze tested whether claimants maintained a stable identity under contradiction.
narrative.present_tension: It literalises possible selves and prepares the Crown's threat to overwrite identity.
narrative.hidden_truth: The Moon Key is the reflection of a key removed from history.
narrative_hooks: Reflections wear different endings.
One reflection already wears the Crown.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Self-doubt made physically present.

nodes.read_aloud: Cold silver walls return your face with tiny errors. Your breath fogs the glass, but one reflection's does not. Footsteps sound ahead before you move.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The mirrors are cold enough to ache through your fingertips. One reflection looks relieved to see you.

nodes.title: Cistern of Eels
nodes.summary: A circular reservoir alive with blind electric eels and drifting scraps of official parchment.

narrative.arrival_text: Black water circles a central platform while parchment scraps drift without becoming wet.
narrative.local_history: The cistern powered document lifts and cooled the ember-salt vaults.
narrative.present_tension: The space ties mundane infrastructure to supernatural administration.
narrative.hidden_truth: The eels fed on residue from oath-charged documents and react violently to unspoken lies.
narrative_hooks: A lie causes the water to spark.
A true admission calms one eel.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Held breath and involuntary anticipation.

nodes.read_aloud: The cistern smells of wet metal and old stone. Black water circles the platform with a slow sucking sound. Blue light snaps beneath the surface, leaving an acrid taste at the back of your mouth.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The water laps against the stone. Ozone prickles in your nose before the next blue flash.

nodes.title: The Taxman's Ledger Room
nodes.summary: Stone shelves hold accounts of tribute, fines, exemptions and debts inherited by the dead.

narrative.arrival_text: Shelves rise into darkness, packed with accounts that continue adding interest in red ink.
narrative.local_history: The room recorded not only money but duties, inherited blame and civic existence.
narrative.present_tension: The ledger fragment makes history actionable by turning narrative evidence into leverage.
narrative.hidden_truth: The records prove the Ninth Guardian was never discharged because officials needed the lock to remain legitimate.
narrative_hooks: Reveal familiar Barrowgate surnames among erased debtors.
A current licence fee mirrors an ancient tithe.
narrative.emotional_undertone: The unease of discovering that nothing has been forgotten.

nodes.read_aloud: Dry leather and old mould thicken the air. Pages turn by themselves in the dark above, a soft, continuous sound like rain heard through a roof.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The page-turning never stops. Red ink glistens wetly on entries centuries old.

nodes.title: Bridge of Sighs
nodes.summary: A narrow bridge over an immeasurable drop. The wind repeats the final words of those who fell.

narrative.arrival_text: The wind speaks in the voices of people who turned back, fell or were pushed.
narrative.local_history: The bridge crossed from administrative levels into enforcement facilities.
narrative.present_tension: Pell's competence and boundary become visible here; assistance is character, not just inventory.
narrative.hidden_truth: The voices include some who survived but left part of themselves behind.
narrative_hooks: Let Pell name knots and risks without heroics.
The bridge repeats a future line in the player's own voice.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Fear made manageable only through attention and trust.

nodes.read_aloud: Wind catches the bridge and sends a tremor through the stone beneath your boots. Voices rise from the drop, some frightened, some resigned. The rope is rough and damp in your hand.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The bridge shivers before you step onto it. One voice below sounds relieved that you came back.

read_aloud_variants.text: Pell tests the damp rope, looks once into the drop and begins choosing anchor points. His attention steadies the scene more than any reassurance could.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Make Pell present before his mechanical assistance is selected.

nodes.title: The Beast Pits
nodes.summary: An arena of rusted gates and old bones. One gate remains closed from the inside.

narrative.arrival_text: The largest gate is dented outward. Something inside has learned that applause means feeding time.
narrative.local_history: Confiscated creatures and political symbols were displayed here as proof of state control.
narrative.present_tension: The fight echoes the player's own position as dangerous asset, criminal or claimant.
narrative.hidden_truth: The beast bears an old collar naming it PROPERTY OF THE CROWN, though the kingdom is gone.
narrative_hooks: The beast may hesitate before an authority seal.
Crowd noise survives without a crowd.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Mutual suspicion between captive and intruder.

nodes.read_aloud: Old blood and animal heat hang over the sand. Chains drag somewhere behind the largest gate. When the unseen creature hears you, the dragging stops; the silence that follows is wary, not empty.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The pit smells of rust and hot fur. Something beyond the gate listens without moving.

nodes.title: Black Wind Forge
nodes.summary: An extinguished forge where cold wind blows from cracks in the anvil.

narrative.arrival_text: Cold wind rises from the anvil, bending every dead flame towards the deep.
narrative.local_history: The forge shaped ember-salt fittings and destroyed seals belonging to disgraced offices.
narrative.present_tension: The Rune-Candle turns hidden institutional truth into visible text.
narrative.hidden_truth: The black wind is heat being drawn towards the Crown rather than ordinary air movement.
narrative_hooks: The candle reveals words on objects that never looked written upon.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Dormant power waiting with unnerving patience.

nodes.read_aloud: The forge is cold, yet wind rises from the anvil and bends every flame towards the floor. Stone soot powders your fingers. The air tastes faintly of burnt salt.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The black wind still pulls downward. Soot gathers in the lines of your hand.

nodes.title: Vault Antechamber
nodes.summary: A nine-sided chamber with doors that open only for recognised authority, recognised debt or overwhelming force.

narrative.arrival_text: Nine walls face nine doors. Each door is labelled with a different answer to the question: WHO MAY ENTER?
narrative.local_history: This chamber tested competing bases of authority: birth, office, debt, conquest, consent, sacrifice, memory, law and witness.
narrative.present_tension: The player must begin combining items and knowledge into a coherent claim.
narrative.hidden_truth: No single answer was intended to be sufficient; the old kingdom failed by pretending otherwise.
narrative_hooks: Let different inventory items illuminate different doors.
narrative.emotional_undertone: The pressure of having to define oneself precisely.

nodes.read_aloud: Nine doors face inward beneath nine different claims of authority. Your words return from each wall in a different tone. The Brass Seal, if you carry it, warms against your body.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The chamber answers your breathing nine times. One door now seems less closed than the others.

nodes.title: Hall of Whispered Debts
nodes.summary: Every unpaid promise ever made in the dungeon is whispered here by a voice that knows the listener's name.

narrative.arrival_text: Voices speak every promise the delver hoped nobody remembered.
narrative.local_history: The hall was designed to prevent officials entering the vault while carrying undisclosed obligations.
narrative.present_tension: Confession can reduce power while denial gives the hall material to use.
narrative.hidden_truth: Some whispered debts belong to previous Crown-wearers and are mistaken for the player's own thoughts.
narrative_hooks: Mix personal, inherited and institutional debts.
A sincere confession should change the emotional temperature.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Shame and defensiveness before exposure.

nodes.read_aloud: The darkness smells faintly of salt and breath. Promises begin at the edge of hearing, then move closer in voices you know. Your mouth goes dry before any of them speaks your name.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The whispers recognise your tread. One promise waits until the others have finished.

nodes.title: The Ossuary Market
nodes.summary: Dead merchants trade memories, names, years of life and occasionally useful equipment.

narrative.arrival_text: Stalls are built from ribs and lacquered boxes. Prices are spoken in birthdays, names and remembered faces.
narrative.local_history: The market formed where identities leaked from drowned records and accumulated around the dead.
narrative.present_tension: Every bargain asks whether memory is property, selfhood or merely another resource.
narrative.hidden_truth: Some merchants are discarded fragments of former Crown-wearers rather than whole dead people.
narrative_hooks: A merchant recognises a memory the player has not yet lost.
Coins are accepted only as curiosities.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Courtesy made unsettling by detachment from ordinary life.
sensory_focus: a smile recalled rather than felt

nodes.read_aloud: Bone chimes stir above narrow stalls. The air carries cold perfume and grave spice. A merchant smiles with polite interest, though the face seems to be remembering how.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The chimes sound softer this time. A stallholder watches you with the patient concern of someone pricing a fragile thing.

nodes.title: Guardian of the Ninth Lock
nodes.summary: A bronze giant kneels before the final seal, awaiting either a challenger or a lawful release.

narrative.arrival_text: The bronze giant kneels with both hands on the sealed door, not to keep others out but to keep itself in place.
narrative.local_history: Varn accepted an emergency debt to hold the Lock until lawful succession. Nobody recorded completion.
narrative.present_tension: Combat is possible, but the richer conflict is whether the player recognises a person inside the office.
narrative.hidden_truth: Varn has counted every year of service and remembers each one separately.
narrative_hooks: Pell's departure should make the threshold feel human before it feels mythic.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Varn: 'State the debt. State the completion. State who will witness release.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Exhaustion dignified into duty.

nodes.read_aloud: The bronze giant kneels with both hands against the sealed door. Heat seeps from the metal into the stone. Its first breath is a low ceremonial hum; when it looks at you, there is no anger in the sound—only endurance.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Bronze warmth reaches you before the Guardian speaks. Dust falls with each measured word.

read_aloud_variants.text: Pell stops several paces from the bronze giant. "This is the Ninth Lock," he says quietly. "My contract ends here."
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Deliver Pell's boundary before routes requiring his departure.

read_aloud_variants.text: The ledger's wording returns to you as Varn speaks. This is not simply a guard at a door; it is an unpaid obligation given a body.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Reflect learned history in the encounter.

nodes.title: Abyssal Stair
nodes.summary: A stair descending around an open void. The lower steps are visible only when not directly observed.

narrative.arrival_text: The stair descends around an absence that seems deeper than the hill can contain.
narrative.local_history: The void is where unrecognised claims and failed identities were metaphysically discarded.
narrative.present_tension: Navigation depends on indirect attention, echoing the theme that systems cannot see what they refuse to recognise.
narrative.hidden_truth: Some lower steps are made from names removed from the archive.
narrative_hooks: Direct observation makes the route disappear.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Dizziness sharpened by distrust of one's own senses.

nodes.read_aloud: Cold air rises from the void and presses against your face. The stair gives no echo at its centre, only the faint flutter of paper falling somewhere too far below.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The next step appears at the edge of your vision. Looking straight at it makes your stomach tighten.

nodes.title: The Sunken Archive
nodes.summary: A drowned archive preserving the legal memory of the lost kingdom beneath black water.

narrative.arrival_text: Shelves continue beneath black water. Words rise from submerged pages like bubbles and vanish at the surface.
narrative.local_history: Custodians flooded the archive to hide evidence of erasure and prevent rival claimants reconstructing succession law.
narrative.present_tension: This is the main explanatory node: it can connect licences, rebels, debts, custodians and Crown without reducing them to exposition.
narrative.hidden_truth: The archive's index recognises the stolen ledger fragment as evidence rather than theft.
narrative_hooks: Reveal facts through documents that implicate named people.
Make every answer create a sharper moral question.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Grief at knowledge preserved too late.

nodes.read_aloud: Black water covers the lower shelves. The room smells of wet stone and old ink. Words rise from submerged pages as silver bubbles, breaking with tiny sounds against the surface.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Cold water laps against the shelves. A sentence surfaces one word at a time, then is gone.

nodes.title: The Gilded Maw
nodes.summary: A trap-lined passage shaped like a golden throat. Its teeth are doors; its tongue is a bridge.

narrative.arrival_text: The passage resembles a golden throat because every door opens inward like a tooth.
narrative.local_history: The Maw tested whether a claimant could recognise consumption disguised as welcome.
narrative.present_tension: The final trap asks whether the player sees treasure as something to take or something that is trying to take them.
narrative.hidden_truth: The mechanism responds to desire: greed makes the passage physically narrower.
narrative_hooks: Descriptions should tighten as the player frames the Crown as possession.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Desire turning gradually into suspicion.

nodes.read_aloud: Warm metal closes around the passage like a throat. The air tastes of salt and old coins. Each hidden mechanism shifts with a soft swallowing click.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The golden walls seem closer. Somewhere ahead, the passage swallows once.

nodes.title: The Warden Below
nodes.summary: The last custodian of the vault, bound to defend the Crown until confronted by stronger authority.

narrative.arrival_text: Serath waits without weapon drawn. The empty space behind the Warden feels more dangerous than armour.
narrative.local_history: Serath was appointed to judge succession after Queen Mereth could no longer be trusted to name an heir.
narrative.present_tension: The confrontation tests accumulated meaning: paperwork, debt, restraint, violence and understanding.
narrative.hidden_truth: Serath wants release but will choose endless duty over a claimant who treats the Crown as loot.
sensory_details: a voice that does not echo
narrative_hooks: Reference the player's route and methods.
Serath should distinguish capability from fitness.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Serath: 'You have proved that doors open for you. Now prove they should.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Severe hope restrained by the fear of another unworthy claimant.
sensory_focus: the weight of a calm evaluating gaze

nodes.read_aloud: Serath waits in a silence so complete that your breathing feels discourteous. Salt sparks drift from the armour. The Warden studies you without hostility and speaks before drawing any weapon. "You entered these ruins as a delver seeking treasure. You stand now in a court of succession. The vault does not ask whether you can take the Crown. It asks what right you have to survive wearing it."
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The silence closes again. Serath's attention settles on what you carry, then on how you carry it.

read_aloud_variants.text: A thin red writ hangs in the air beside Serath. The Warden has already received the Registrar's accusation.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Carry the adverse record into the final judgement.

read_aloud_variants.text: You recognise the structure of Serath's questions: authority, acknowledged debt and restraint. The archive did not describe a battle. It described this judgement.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Reward the archive route with clear interpretive context.

read_aloud_variants.text: Serath's gaze settles on the Brass Seal. For the first time, the Warden addresses you as a claimant rather than an intruder.

mandatory_exposition.text: The final confrontation is a succession judgement, not merely a treasure-guarding battle.
mandatory_exposition.player_takeaway: Items, reputation, recovered debts, restraint and recognised authority are evidence supporting a claim.

nodes.title: Vault of the Mythic Treasure
nodes.summary: A silent chamber containing one pedestal, one crown and the accumulated heat of a fallen dynasty.

narrative.arrival_text: The chamber contains no coins, only the Crown and the heat of promises still waiting to be fulfilled.
narrative.local_history: The vault was intentionally emptied so no lesser treasure could confuse succession with reward.
narrative.present_tension: Expectation collapses into a single irreversible decision.
narrative.hidden_truth: The room itself is a witness; any declaration made here becomes part of the Crown's memory.
narrative_hooks: Ask what the delver expected wealth to solve.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Expectation collapsing into awe and apprehension.

nodes.read_aloud: The vault is dry and warm, smelling faintly of rain on hot stone. There are no heaps of coin. Only the Crown rests on its pedestal. The emptiness is deliberate: this chamber was built to prevent any claimant confusing wealth with the right to rule. Whatever you do next will decide whether you leave with treasure, inherit the ruins, or become another voice inside the Crown.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Heat gathers on your skin. The Crown's red light seems to pulse with your breathing.

mandatory_exposition.text: The Crown offers three distinct outcomes: possession resisted, identity consumed, or rulership accepted.
mandatory_exposition.player_takeaway: The final routes are choices about identity and responsibility, not merely different reward tiers.

nodes.title: The Crown of Ember Salt
nodes.summary: The mythic treasure of the Rust Wind Hills. It offers sovereignty before it offers safety.

narrative.arrival_text: The Crown does not speak aloud. It remembers the delver's own voice and uses that instead.
narrative.local_history: Forged from oath-bearing crystal, it contains impressions of every recognised sovereign of Veyr.
narrative.present_tension: The treasure offers real power through an intimate assault on the distinction between role and self.
narrative.hidden_truth: Queen Mereth is the strongest preserved identity, but not the only one—and not necessarily the first voice the player hears.
narrative_hooks: Replay earlier promises in altered meanings.
Let resisted, bound and mastered outcomes feel distinct.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Mereth: 'You may keep your name. I require only everything it can command.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Temptation made intimate rather than grand.

nodes.read_aloud: Heat gathers behind your eyes before you touch the Crown. Salt sharpens on your lips. When it speaks, it uses your own voice with such tenderness that for a moment you want to trust it.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The red facets hold faces that are not quite reflections. Your own voice waits among them.

nodes.title: Escape, Riches in Hand
nodes.summary: The delver returns to Barrowgate wealthy, famous and sufficiently alive to become unreliable in taverns.

narrative.arrival_text: Barrowgate celebrates the return before anyone agrees what the Crown is worth.
narrative.local_history: No previous claimant removed the Crown while resisting formal succession.
narrative.present_tension: Freedom is possible, but the player now carries an object that makes every public promise politically dangerous.
narrative.hidden_truth: The town's licence clerks may recognise the Crown as superior authority even if the player refuses it.
sensory_details: wax seals softening near the Crown
narrative_hooks: End on a small promise accidentally becoming binding.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Pell: 'Tell me you brought treasure, not government.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Relief complicated almost immediately by doubt.

nodes.read_aloud: Barrowgate's bells begin before you reach the gate. The crowd smells of rain, wool and hot wax. Pell, if he waits there, lets out a breath and then looks at the Crown with quiet concern.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The town celebrates, though people lower their voices whenever the Crown catches the light.

read_aloud_variants.text: Pell is waiting above the Lock and returns with you. His relief lasts only until he sees how the Crown catches the light.
read_aloud_variants.purpose: Pay off Pell's decision to remain as a living witness.

nodes.title: Cursed Possession
nodes.summary: The Crown leaves the dungeon wearing the delver rather than the other way around.

narrative.arrival_text: The returning figure uses the delver's face perfectly and their habits almost perfectly.
narrative.local_history: The Crown has absorbed rulers before, but each new identity changes the thing doing the absorbing.
narrative.present_tension: Victory and loss become difficult to separate.
narrative.hidden_truth: Fragments of the original delver may still influence how the new sovereign rules.
narrative_hooks: Use one beloved mannerism at the wrong emotional moment.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Recognition undermined by a quiet, irreversible estrangement.

nodes.read_aloud: The figure returning to Barrowgate has your stride and your voice. It even smiles at the right people. Only the pauses are wrong, as though someone older is waiting for each memory to explain itself.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: Those who knew you best cannot name what changed. They simply stop speaking as freely in your presence.

nodes.title: Claim the Dungeon Lordship
nodes.summary: The delver becomes lawful ruler of the ruins, inheriting their wealth, monsters, debts and licensing obligations.

narrative.arrival_text: The dungeon's sealed doors open like a city waking. Every surviving mechanism asks for instruction.
narrative.local_history: A lawful successor has not been recognised since the locking of the Crown.
narrative.present_tension: The ending grants power while inheriting every unresolved debt, prisoner, monster and administrative absurdity.
narrative.hidden_truth: Mastery is not immunity: the office will continue pressing the claimant towards older patterns.
narrative_hooks: List three immediate petitions before any celebration.
Barrowgate sends a tax demand to its new sovereign.
narrative.voice_or_prompt: Serath: 'Then rule. The ruins have waited long enough to become your excuses.'
narrative.emotional_undertone: Triumph giving way to the weight of immediate responsibility.

nodes.read_aloud: Locks release through the dungeon in a rolling thunder that can be felt through the soles of your feet. Then come the voices—clerks, prisoners, mechanisms and the dead—each waiting for your first answer.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The ruins remain awake around you. Somewhere, a bell rings for a petition nobody has heard in centuries.

nodes.title: Indefinite Administrative Detention
nodes.summary: The delver's adventure ends in a cell while the appeal is referred to a committee that no longer exists.

narrative.arrival_text: The cell door remains closed while generations of forms move from tray to tray.
narrative.local_history: Many prisoners were never sentenced; they simply became unresolved cases.
narrative.present_tension: The joke curdles into institutional horror.
narrative.hidden_truth: The delver's legal identity may persist in the dungeon long after their body fails.
narrative_hooks: End with a courteous notice that the case remains active.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Hope eroded by impersonal delay.
sensory_focus: fresh ink on a useless notice

nodes.read_aloud: The corridor stays cold. Paper slides from tray to tray beyond the bars, and each polite little sound makes hope feel more embarrassing.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: A notice arrives smelling of fresh ink. It confirms that your case remains active.

nodes.title: A Delver's Memorial Plaque
nodes.summary: Barrowgate adds another small brass name beneath the licensing hall stairs.

narrative.arrival_text: Barrowgate engraves a small brass plaque after deducting the fee from any recoverable estate.
narrative.local_history: The memorial tradition began as mutual aid and became part civic ritual, part advertising.
narrative.present_tension: Death feeds the economy and the myth without resolving either.
narrative.hidden_truth: The dungeon may retain an echo of the delver if they died near ember-salt.
narrative_hooks: Echo an earlier promise or rumour in the memorial wording.
narrative.emotional_undertone: Private grief absorbed into public routine.

nodes.read_aloud: A hammer taps your name into brass while red dust moves across the square. Someone who knew you presses their thumb into the new letters, then draws their hand away before anyone sees.
nodes.read_aloud_revisit: The plaque cools beneath the gatehouse stairs. Another expedition forms its queue nearby.
