semantic_layer.purpose: Provide machine-readable narrative meaning beyond topology. Entries are intended for semantic retrieval, contextual narration, consequence generation, foreshadowing and consistent improvisation.

interpretation_guidance.surface_vs_truth: Prefer surface beliefs early in play. Reveal hidden truths only when supported by knowledge, items, locations or accumulated narrative evidence.
interpretation_guidance.semantic_reuse: When narrating a node, combine its local narrative fields with linked motifs, threads, factions, characters and world truths rather than repeating the summary verbatim.
interpretation_guidance.continuity: Use state_semantics, route continuity references and knowledge catalogue entries to make prior choices visible in later descriptions.
interpretation_guidance.ambiguity: Rumours with mixed or false truth values should remain useful: even false claims reveal beliefs, interests or fears held by their source.
interpretation_guidance.sensory_restraint: Use one or two specific sensory impressions at a time. Prefer details that reveal place, danger or meaning over decorative lists. Across a sequence of scenes, vary sight, sound, smell, touch and taste.
interpretation_guidance.emotional_restraint: Show emotion through hesitation, posture, breath, hands, eye contact, small changes of tone or what a person chooses not to say. Avoid naming an emotion when behaviour can carry it.
interpretation_guidance.embodied_continuity: Let conditions and environments remain physically present: wet sleeves stay cold, iron leaves a taste, long corridors alter sound, fatigue changes how choices feel.

narrative_predicates.believes: The subject treats the object as true, whether or not it is.
narrative_predicates.conceals: The subject intentionally obscures the object.
narrative_predicates.echoes: The subject repeats, mirrors or satirises the object.
narrative_predicates.foreshadows: The subject prepares the meaning or imagery of the object.
narrative_predicates.legitimises: The subject grants recognised authority within a social or magical system.
narrative_predicates.inherits: The subject receives an unresolved duty, debt, title or consequence from the object.
narrative_predicates.contradicts: The subject provides evidence against the object.
narrative_predicates.exploits: The subject gains power or profit from the object.
narrative_predicates.binds: The subject places an enforceable obligation upon the object.
narrative_predicates.remembers: The subject preserves identity, testimony or experience belonging to the object.
narrative_predicates.erases: The subject removes a person, debt or fact from recognised history.
narrative_predicates.humanises: The subject makes an abstract system visible through individual cost.
narrative_predicates.corrupts: The subject changes a role, promise or institution away from its claimed purpose.
narrative_predicates.resists: The subject opposes the logic or authority of the object.
narrative_predicates.completes: The subject resolves or fulfils the object.
narrative_predicates.reframes: The subject changes how the object should be understood.

historical_eras.name: The Ember Regency
historical_eras.summary: The hill kingdom of Veyr grew wealthy by mining ember-salt, a red crystal that records spoken oaths. Its rulers slowly replaced trust with enforceable magical administration.

historical_eras.name: The Building of the Ninth Treasury
historical_eras.summary: Queen Mereth IX ordered a subterranean treasury, archive, court and prison beneath the hills. The complex was designed to protect the Crown and to decide who possessed lawful authority.

historical_eras.name: The Salt Revolt
historical_eras.summary: Debtors, miners and erased citizens rebelled. Ilya of the Blind Road smuggled families through maintenance passages while officials continued recording fines against settlements already burning.

historical_eras.name: The Locking of the Crown
historical_eras.summary: The final custodians sealed the Crown rather than let any faction claim it. Their offices, debts and instructions persisted after their bodies and kingdom failed.

historical_eras.name: The Barrowgate Settlement
historical_eras.summary: A frontier town formed around salvage from the ruins. After repeated disasters, the council created a licensing scheme using brass seal fragments found in the old quarry.

factions.name: Barrowgate Civic Compact
factions.public_goal: Keep dungeon access orderly, taxable and survivable enough to sustain the town.
factions.private_pressure: The town budget now depends on licences, fines, inns, funerals and recovered treasure.
beliefs: A delver who paid the fee has freely accepted the risk.
The dungeon is a municipal asset because nobody stronger has successfully claimed it.
factions.contradiction: Its seal derives from the old treasury, so its authority is less invented—and more dangerous—than the council understands.

factions.name: The Red Quill
factions.public_goal: Provide affordable paperwork to those excluded by Barrowgate's fees.
factions.private_pressure: Its members descend from families erased from the old ledgers and regard official records as weapons.
beliefs: A document is only sacred to the person it protects.
Forgery can restore a truth that official history removed.
factions.contradiction: The Red Quill profits from the same exclusion it condemns.

factions.name: Clergy of the Last Cinder
factions.public_goal: Prepare delvers to meet death without lying to themselves.
factions.private_pressure: The clergy suspect the Crown traps identity but lack proof and fear provoking the town council.
beliefs: A freely admitted debt has less power than a hidden one.
Names spoken over ash cannot be wholly possessed.
The Crown is not evil; it is obedience without mercy.
factions.contradiction: The temple accepts payment for blessings against a system it claims should not be transactional.

factions.name: The Way of the Dust Saint
factions.public_goal: Remember Ilya, patron of hidden roads and lost people.
factions.private_pressure: Most worshippers know only fragments of Ilya's role in the Salt Revolt.
beliefs: A hidden road is righteous when the official road serves injustice.
The lost remain real even when records deny them.
Escape can be a form of victory.

factions.name: The Rust Wind Toll Union
factions.public_goal: Collect a fair toll from travellers using goblin-maintained paths.
factions.private_pressure: The goblins imitate Barrowgate because stamped authority discourages violence more effectively than threats.
beliefs: A barrier becomes lawful when enough people stop at it.
Receipts are armour made of paper.
Humans respect nonsense when it has a seal.

factions.name: Custodians of the Ninth Treasury
factions.public_goal: Preserve the Crown and recognise only lawful succession.
factions.private_pressure: No living sovereign remains, but their instructions contain no expiry clause.
beliefs: Office is more durable than the office-holder.
Correct procedure is a moral defence against ambition.
An unrecognised claimant is indistinguishable from a thief.

characters.name: Pell Vane
characters.role: Torchbearer and human measure of danger
characters.wants: Earn enough to buy his younger sister out of an apprenticeship contract.
characters.fears: Becoming another unnamed hireling in a delver's heroic story.
knows: How to anchor a line across the Bridge of Sighs.
That the air changes near the Ninth Lock.
That several famous delvers quietly turned back.
characters.voice: Practical, dry and unimpressed by dramatic speeches.
sample_lines: A torch is not courage. It just lets courage see what it has agreed to.
My contract says the Ninth Lock. It does not say beyond the Ninth Lock.
Heroes always call it retreat after the rest of us have called it survival.
emotional_tells: Warms his fingers around a lamp before discussing danger.
Checks knots while other people boast.
When worried, becomes more precise rather than more expressive.

characters.name: Ollis Marr
characters.role: Senior clerk of the Licensing Hall
characters.wants: Make the licensing system defensible, solvent and impossible to blame on him personally.
characters.fears: Discovering that the dungeon has been treating his signatures as royal decrees.
sample_lines: The indemnity does not prevent death. It prevents dispute about whose death it is.
Please initial beside 'monstrous transformation'. The category includes undeath.
emotional_tells: Smooths the same edge of a document when uncertain.
Looks towards the seal press before answering questions about authority.
Relief appears as a slightly quicker stamp, never a smile.

characters.name: Maera Voss, called the Red Quill
characters.role: Forger, agitator and keeper of erased names
characters.wants: Recover proof that the old kingdom deliberately erased rebel families from legal existence.
characters.fears: That forged identity eventually becomes another cage.
characters.voice: Precise, amused and sharper than her knives.
sample_lines: A false permit can describe a true person. Official records manage the reverse every day.
The ink is counterfeit. The exclusion is entirely genuine.
emotional_tells: Studies hands before faces.
Turns guarded amusement into a raised eyebrow.
Anger shows in how carefully she aligns names on the page.

characters.name: Ilya of the Blind Road
characters.role: Dead rebel guide later reduced to a harmless roadside saint
characters.wants: Historically: move people beyond the reach of the treasury's records.
characters.legacy: Ilya's hidden routes survive, but the reason for them has been softened into folklore about lost travellers.
emotional_tells: Remembered in carvings with one hand extended behind, as if guiding someone unseen.
Later devotional images remove the exhaustion visible in the oldest reliefs.

characters.name: Orrick, the Rat-King
characters.role: Swarm intelligence formed around the memories of the last quartermaster
characters.wants: Keep possession of every key because surrendering them feels like dying again.
characters.fears: The silence that follows when the swarm stops repeating Orrick's name.
characters.voice: Plural, acquisitive and occasionally heartbreakingly formal.
sample_lines: We are Quartermaster Orrick. We have counted ourselves. We are sufficient.
Keys are names for doors. We keep the names.
emotional_tells: The swarm draws closer around the little crown whenever its identity is questioned.
Formal language frays into chittering when afraid.
Keys are handled with the tenderness of keepsakes.

characters.name: Registrar Halvek
characters.role: Undead inspector bound to the last valid procedure
characters.wants: Complete one legally unambiguous inspection and receive a lawful discharge.
characters.fears: Discretion, because discretion made him complicit in the Salt Revolt's purges.
sample_lines: Compassion is not listed among the permitted grounds for variance.
Your document is valid. Your authority is not. These are separate defects.
emotional_tells: Stillness becomes more rigid when a rule creates an unjust result.
Reorders paper rather than acknowledge uncertainty.
A pause before judgement is the nearest thing Halvek permits himself to dread.

characters.name: Varn of the Ninth Lock
characters.role: Bronze guardian and guarantor of the treasury's final debt
characters.wants: Have the old debt acknowledged so that obedience can become completion.
characters.fears: Being destroyed before anyone understands what it guarded.
characters.voice: Ceremonial and slow, as though every sentence is an oath.
emotional_tells: Dust falls more quickly when old debts are named.
Never raises its voice; strain appears as heat through the bronze.
Release is met first with disbelief, then a single unguarded breath.

characters.name: Serath, the Warden Below
characters.wants: End the vacancy without releasing the Crown to someone incapable of resisting it.
characters.fears: A claimant who mistakes possession for sovereignty.
characters.voice: Calm, judicial and older than anger.
sample_lines: A crown is not a prize. It is a promise with an audience.
You have reached the vault. That proves appetite, not fitness.
emotional_tells: Looks at how objects are carried before asking what they are.
Disappointment makes the voice gentler, not harsher.
Hope appears only as a fractional lowering of the weapon.

characters.name: Queen Mereth IX
characters.role: Last sovereign of Veyr and strongest personality preserved in the Crown
characters.wants: Continue ruling by making a willing claimant indistinguishable from herself.
characters.fears: Being remembered merely as the ruler who lost the kingdom.
sample_lines: You came for treasure because the word 'throne' frightened you.
Wear me, and every promise made in your hearing can become a wall.
emotional_tells: Uses tenderness when command would provoke resistance.
Mirrors the claimant's own cadence to create intimacy.
Impatience appears as memories arriving before they are invited.

motifs.name: Paper, seals and performed legitimacy
motifs.meaning: Authority often begins as a shared pretence and becomes real through repeated recognition.

motifs.name: Debt outliving the debtor
motifs.meaning: Unresolved obligations become architecture, monsters and inherited roles.

motifs.name: Names as proof of existence
motifs.meaning: To be recorded is to be recognised, but to be recorded is also to become governable.

motifs.name: The moral ambiguity of hidden roads
motifs.meaning: Secret routes can be cowardice, crime, resistance or mercy depending on who controls the official path.

motifs.name: Work continuing after purpose has died
motifs.meaning: Institutions become monstrous when process survives the reason it was created.

motifs.name: Light as witness rather than safety
motifs.meaning: Light reveals obligations and truths; it does not necessarily make them survivable.

motifs.name: Every crown is also a cage
motifs.meaning: Power enlarges the self's reach while narrowing the self's freedom.

narrative_threads.name: The Licence That Works
narrative_threads.initial_question: Why does an ancient dungeon recognise a modern town permit?
narrative_threads.surface_explanation: Barrowgate has mapped and tamed the public approaches.
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: The licence seal reproduces a fragment of Veyr's succession magic.

narrative_threads.name: The People Removed from the Ledger
narrative_threads.initial_question: Why does the Red Quill care about a dead kingdom's accounts?
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: The old treasury legally erased rebel families, making confiscation and execution procedurally invisible.

narrative_threads.name: Pell's Line
narrative_threads.initial_question: Is a hired torchbearer equipment, companion or witness?
narrative_threads.surface_explanation: Pell provides light and practical assistance.
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: His refusal at the Ninth Lock creates a moral boundary the delver may ignore but cannot pretend not to see.

narrative_threads.name: The Blind Road
narrative_threads.initial_question: Why are the saint's shrines positioned beside hidden entrances?
narrative_threads.surface_explanation: Travellers prayed there before dangerous journeys.
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: Ilya's cult preserves escape infrastructure from the Salt Revolt.

narrative_threads.name: The Custodians Want Release
narrative_threads.initial_question: Why do the dungeon's officials permit alternatives to combat?
narrative_threads.surface_explanation: They are rigid machines with exploitable rules.
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: Halvek, Varn and Serath each seek a lawful end to duties they cannot abandon.

narrative_threads.name: The Crown Remembers
narrative_threads.initial_question: What makes the Crown more than valuable metal?
narrative_threads.surface_explanation: It is the lost regalia of a rich kingdom.
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: Ember-salt stores sovereign personality and makes witnessed promises binding.

narrative_threads.name: Everyone Invents a Toll Gate
narrative_threads.initial_question: Why do goblins, townsfolk and undead all organise danger through paperwork?
narrative_threads.deeper_truth: The Crown's buried logic leaks outward: recognition, debt and stamped authority reshape behaviour above it.

world_truths.claim: Ember-salt crystal records spoken oaths and the identity of recognised speakers.
implications: Witnessed promises can become supernatural constraints.

world_truths.claim: Barrowgate's licence seal was cut from a fragment of the Ninth Treasury's brass authority seal.
implications: Modern licences create weak but genuine authority.
The town is participating in succession magic.

world_truths.claim: The dungeon was a treasury, archive, prison and succession court rather than a simple fortress.
The old regime erased rebel families from legal records before confiscating their property and lives.
Ilya of the Blind Road was a rebel organiser, not merely a patron saint of travellers.
The surviving custodians are capable of desire and guilt but cannot act outside their offices.
The Ossuary Market exists because dead identities leak from the Crown's archive of remembered rulers.
The Rat-King is partly the preserved memory of Quartermaster Orrick distributed through the swarm.
The Crown grants authority by narrowing the wearer's identity around the role of sovereign.

rumours.text: The treasure is a crown that glows hot enough to melt a liar's teeth.
rumours.useful_truth: The Crown reacts to spoken promises and false claims.

rumours.text: No hired hand crosses the Ninth Lock twice.
rumours.useful_truth: Survivors recognise the Ninth Lock as the boundary beyond which roles become binding.

rumours.text: A red feather painted under a windowsill marks someone who can make the council believe anything.
rumours.useful_truth: The Red Quill forges documents and preserves erased identities.

rumours.text: The Dust Saint closes one eye so lost travellers can pass unseen beneath the other.
rumours.useful_truth: Shrines mark routes once used to evade the old treasury.

rumours.text: The iron official cannot read a permit held upside down.
rumours.useful_truth: People prefer comic flaws to understanding the Registrar's actual rules.

rumours.text: The dead below will buy your childhood if you can describe it accurately.
rumours.usual_source: A retired delver with no memory of his mother
rumours.useful_truth: The Ossuary Market trades in memory and identity.

rumours.text: The Warden has killed every claimant because none remembered to ask permission.
rumours.usual_source: A copied fragment of an old expedition journal
rumours.useful_truth: The Warden judges legitimacy rather than merely guarding treasure.

rumours.text: Barrowgate earns more from failed delves than successful ones.
rumours.useful_truth: The town economy has adapted to repeated loss.

rumour_crown_ember_salt.statement: The Crown responds to spoken claims and may punish false sovereignty.

rumour_ninth_lock.statement: Experienced hirelings treat the Ninth Lock as a meaningful boundary.

rumour_red_quill.statement: A covert forger operates in Barrowgate under the sign of a red quill.

rumour_dust_saint.statement: The Dust Saint's shrines encode hidden paths.

map_three_entrances.statement: The complex has three meaningful approaches: official, concealed and flooded.

hidden_shaft_location.statement: The Dust Saint shrine marks the concealed shaft behind the upper garrison.

rat_tunnels.statement: The rat tunnels connect the former stores to the surveyor's chamber.

loose_stone_bridge.statement: A loose stone beside the Bridge of Sighs conceals a forge passage.

registrar_values_procedure.statement: The Registrar may accept recognised procedure even when it dislikes the claimant.

debt_of_ninth_guardian.statement: The treasury never discharged Varn's service debt after the locking of the Crown.

the_dead_trade_in_names.statement: Names and memories function as currency among the dead because recognised identity has power below.

guardian_true_name.statement: Varn's complete ceremonial name grants leverage but does not itself settle the debt.

lawful_release_phrase.statement: A recognised seal-holder may discharge a custodian whose defined obligation has been completed.

warden_bound_by_crown_law.statement: Serath must recognise a claimant who combines valid authority, acknowledged debt and demonstrated restraint.

pell_waits_above_ninth_lock.statement: Pell refused the final descent but remains a living witness to the delver's return or disappearance.

last_cinder_blessing.narrative_meaning: The delver has publicly admitted mortality and carries ash marked with their true name.
narration_cues: The character is harder for the Crown to wholly redefine.

soaked.narrative_meaning: Clothes, gear and papers carry the black mineral water of the old cisterns.
narration_cues: Cold becomes a persistent physical nuisance.

spore_dreams.narrative_meaning: The cloister fungus causes borrowed memories and uncertainty over which thoughts belong to the delver.
narration_cues: Use first-person memories the character never lived.
Mirrors may answer before the delver speaks.

missing_year.narrative_meaning: A coherent year of the delver's life has been traded away and no longer feels personally owned.
narration_cues: Familiar skills may feel learned from a stranger.
Personal timelines contain a clean, disturbing gap.

permit_status.none: The dungeon treats the delver as unrecognised and therefore presumptively hostile.
permit_status.licensed: The delver carries weak but genuine recognised authority derived from Barrowgate's seal.
permit_status.counterfeit: The delver carries a document that imitates authority but may still resonate unpredictably with old systems.
permit_status.revoked: The delver has been formally marked as an invalid or hostile claimant.

registrar_alerted.false: The old administrative systems have not formed a coherent adverse record against the delver.
registrar_alerted.true: The delver is now represented in the dungeon's surviving bureaucracy as a suspected trespasser, forger or violent claimant. Later officials may know the accusation without knowing the facts.

rat_king_dead.false: Orrick's swarm identity still occupies and interprets the warren.
rat_king_dead.true: The swarm has collapsed into ordinary rats and scattered memories. Keys, paths and scraps remain, but the voice calling itself Orrick is gone.

ninth_lock_open.false: The final custodial debt remains active and the lower succession route is sealed.
ninth_lock_open.true: The old obligation has been defeated, discharged or compelled into temporary compliance.

crown_claimed.false: The throne remains vacant and the Crown continues waiting for a recognised self.
crown_claimed.true: The Crown has attached itself to a claimant and begun rewriting the meaning of their promises.

crown_attunement.none: No relationship with the Crown has formed.
crown_attunement.resisted: The claimant has taken the Crown while preserving a meaningful boundary between self and office.
crown_attunement.bound: The claimant's identity is being overwritten by the Crown's accumulated sovereign personalities.
crown_attunement.mastered: The claimant has accepted office while forcing the Crown to recognise their distinct identity.

rumour_crown_ember_salt.title: The Crown Rumour
rumour_crown_ember_salt.player_text: The treasure is said to be a crown that reacts to spoken claims. The warning sounds embellished, but several survivors agree that promises matter in the deep dungeon.
rumour_crown_ember_salt.meaning: Foreshadows oath-magic and the Crown's interest in identity.

rumour_ninth_lock.title: The Ninth Lock Warning
rumour_ninth_lock.player_text: Experienced hirelings treat the Ninth Lock as the boundary beyond which ordinary contracts and good sense stop protecting anyone.
rumour_ninth_lock.meaning: Prepares Pell's departure and the shift into the mythic final descent.

rumour_red_quill.title: The Red Quill
rumour_red_quill.player_text: A covert forger operates in Barrowgate. A small red feather painted beneath a window marks the way.
rumour_red_quill.meaning: Makes the forgery route discoverable and links it to erased identities.

rumour_dust_saint.title: The Blind Road
rumour_dust_saint.player_text: Shrines to the Dust Saint are said to mark hidden roads. The old stories may preserve practical directions.
rumour_dust_saint.meaning: Makes the shrine and hidden shaft feel earned rather than arbitrary.

map_three_entrances.title: Three Ways Below
map_three_entrances.player_text: From the watchtower you identify three approaches: the public quarry stair, a concealed shaft, and a flooded service tunnel leading beneath the upper checkpoints.
map_three_entrances.meaning: Frames entrance choice as official recognition, secrecy or physical risk.

hidden_shaft_location.title: The Saint's Hidden Shaft
hidden_shaft_location.player_text: The shrine's mechanism opens a concealed shaft behind the old garrison rooms. The saint's symbol was a route marker, not merely decoration.
hidden_shaft_location.meaning: Confirms Ilya's shrines as preserved escape infrastructure.

rat_tunnels.title: The Rat Tunnels
rat_tunnels.player_text: The warren's narrow stores connect to the dead surveyor's chamber. The Rat-King has been guarding more than food.
rat_tunnels.meaning: Connects Orrick's old office to the mapmaker and provides spatial continuity.

loose_stone_bridge.title: The Loose Stone
loose_stone_bridge.player_text: The map identifies a loose stone beside the Bridge of Sighs. Behind it lies a maintenance passage to the Black Wind Forge.
loose_stone_bridge.meaning: Turns the bridge's secret route into a learned fact.

registrar_values_procedure.title: The Registrar's Constraint
registrar_values_procedure.player_text: The chapel's surviving vows reveal that the Iron Registrar must honour recognised procedure, even when it dislikes the person invoking it.
registrar_values_procedure.meaning: Explains why procedural knowledge can replace licence or combat.

debt_of_ninth_guardian.title: Varn's Unpaid Service
debt_of_ninth_guardian.player_text: The ledger shows that Varn was ordered to hold the Ninth Lock until succession was resolved. The treasury deliberately left the obligation open so the seal could never be lawfully abandoned.
debt_of_ninth_guardian.meaning: Reframes the Guardian as a person trapped inside an unsettled duty.

the_dead_trade_in_names.title: The Currency of the Dead
the_dead_trade_in_names.player_text: Below the treasury, a recognised name has power. The dead trade memories and identities because official existence remains valuable here.
the_dead_trade_in_names.meaning: Explains the Ossuary Market and links memory to the Crown's authority.

guardian_true_name.title: Varn's Ceremonial Name
guardian_true_name.player_text: You learn the Guardian's complete ceremonial name. Speaking it can compel attention, but it does not settle the obligation that binds Varn to the Lock.
guardian_true_name.meaning: Provides leverage while distinguishing coercion from release.

lawful_release_phrase.title: The Words of Discharge
lawful_release_phrase.player_text: A recognised seal-holder may release a custodian whose stated duty has been completed. The words require the debt, its completion and a witness to be named.
lawful_release_phrase.meaning: Makes the peaceful Guardian resolution intelligible.

warden_bound_by_crown_law.title: The Warden's Test
warden_bound_by_crown_law.player_text: Serath must recognise a claimant who can show authority, acknowledge the old debts and demonstrate restraint. The Warden guards succession, not treasure alone.
warden_bound_by_crown_law.meaning: Makes the final non-combat routes and succession reveal coherent.

pell_waits_above_ninth_lock.title: A Living Witness
pell_waits_above_ninth_lock.player_text: Pell will not cross the Ninth Lock, but he waits above it. Someone living will know whether you return—and what you bring back.
pell_waits_above_ninth_lock.meaning: Preserves Pell's emotional continuity into the ending.

r001.success_text: Inside the Bent Nail, a survivor lowers his cup when he hears where you mean to go.

r002.success_text: Every item on the stalls has already failed to save someone else.

r003.success_text: The workers assess your boots, your hands and whether you listen when they answer.

r004.success_text: The priest's kindness is practised, but not empty.

r005.success_text: The clerk's pen pauses above the line marked NEXT OF KIN.

r006.success_text: A red feather beneath a windowsill points towards paperwork the council never issued.

r007.success_text: Beyond the last signpost, Barrowgate can still charge you—but it can no longer help.

r008.success_text: You leave the Bent Nail with four useful threads: the Crown listens to promises, hirelings fear the Ninth Lock, the Red Quill forges permits, and the Dust Saint marks hidden roads.

r009.success_text: Five gold buys rope, oil, provisions and a little more time below. The weight of the pack is reassuring until you notice the former owner's initials.

r010.success_text: The merchant watches you leave, then turns the best rope back towards the wall.

r011.success_text: Pell signs the contract and tests your spare lamp without asking permission. He will carry light and secure one dangerous crossing, but his line ends at the Ninth Lock.

r012.success_text: Pell nods once, neither offended nor reassured.

r013.success_text: Two gold changes hands. The priest marks your brow with warm ash, quietly repeats your name, and asks you not to make promises underground unless you mean them.

r014.success_text: The priest does not curse you; somehow that feels less reassuring.

r015.success_text: The licence receives its wax seal. For an instant the paper feels heavier than it should, as though something beneath the hills has acknowledged the issue.

r016.success_text: The clerk does not look at you when he gives the hint; that is how you know it matters.
r016.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Barrowgate.

r017.success_text: The clerk closes your file with the tenderness of someone preparing evidence.

r018.success_text: Maera gives you a convincing permit and points out the tiny deliberate flaw. "A forgery should know what it is," she says.

r019.success_text: Maera burns the unfinished permit before the wax has cooled.

r020.success_text: The cairns sing before you are close enough to blame the wind.

r021.success_text: The shortest road ends at a barrier freshly painted with the word LEGAL.

r022.success_text: The ruined tower promises perspective, not safety.

r023.success_text: The chipped treasury mark points towards the route most people were meant to use.

r024.success_text: A dented bell rings once, though nobody has touched it.

r025.success_text: Warm dust gathers against the wind, sketching the shape of a half-closed eye.

r026.success_text: The goblin clerk's chest lifts slightly when you ask for a receipt.

r027.success_text: The goblin's suspicion competes visibly with his desire to be treated as an official.
r027.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Goblin Toll Path.

r028.success_text: The barrier is light enough to lift and heavy enough to start a war over.
r028.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r029.success_text: From the broken tower, the dungeon resolves into three approaches: public stair, hidden shaft and flooded service tunnel.

r030.success_text: Old charcoal marks turn a collapsed wall into a sentence only the equipped can read.

r031.success_text: The channel below shines black, and something in it flashes blue.

r032.success_text: The coin drops sideways inside the saint's blind eye. Stone shifts, revealing the hidden shaft, and a small clay token comes free with the mechanism.

r033.success_text: The shrine keeps its secret without reproach.

r034.success_text: Each seventh stair bears a fee no living clerk still collects.

r035.success_text: The shaft is narrow enough to make every piece of equipment feel like a confession.

r036.success_text: The black water has no current, yet it keeps pulling towards the archive numbers.
r036.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Cistern of Eels.

r037.success_text: The Gate of Tithes opens three slots and waits to learn what kind of person you are.

r038.success_text: Your boots strike stone beside a skeleton still arguing with its own map.

r039.success_text: The gate accepts the licence with more respect than the town clerk did.

r040.success_text: The counterfeit seal warms, hesitates and asks no questions you can hear.
r040.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to The Delvers' Gaol.

r041.success_text: Behind the grille, many small voices are counting your tools.
r041.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Gate of Tithes.

r042.success_text: The gate values old coin more reliably than modern courage.

r043.success_text: Metal chimes ahead, accompanied by the whisper: inventory, inventory, inventory.

r044.success_text: The officers' stair has collapsed in exactly the places an officer would once have stood.

r045.success_text: A procession carved into the wall turns aside where the official corridor continues.

r046.success_text: The swarm loses grammar before it loses life. The last rat releases the Brass Seal. What remains in the warren is ordinary movement and a little crown nobody touches.
r046.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r047.success_text: The swarm accepts the food and your use of Orrick's title. In return it reveals the store tunnels leading to the mapmaker's chamber.

r048.success_text: The Brass Seal completes a shape in the chains, and the dry well begins to rise.

r049.success_text: The voice below files your departure under INCOMPLETE.

r050.success_text: The line on the shield only makes sense when followed without looking ahead.

r051.success_text: A route scratched across solid metal becomes a doorway when viewed from the corner of the eye.

r052.success_text: The chapel's vows reveal a surviving rule: the Registrar must honour recognised procedure, even when the result offends its judgement.

r053.success_text: Beneath the altar, saints and smugglers have used the same handholds.

r054.success_text: Halvek stamps the licence. The sound travels down the corridor, followed by several distant locks accepting the decision.

r055.success_text: The counterfeit survives the inspection. Halvek returns it without comment, but the pause behind the visor suggests the document has not been entirely believed.
r055.failure_text: The seal fails under Halvek's scrutiny. The permit is marked invalid, an adverse record is opened, and iron hands direct you towards the gaol.

r056.success_text: For one instant Halvek looks less like a machine than a tired man behind one.
r056.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to The Delvers' Gaol.

r057.success_text: The Registrar falls and its iron key becomes yours. The papers continue moving, and somewhere deeper the system records violence without recording why.
r057.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r058.success_text: The service lock recognises the key without asking who now carries it.

r059.success_text: The condemned drain was built for waste, not people, which may be why nobody watches it.
r059.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Indefinite Administrative Detention.

r060.success_text: A courteous notice promises review by a committee whose members are all dust.

r061.success_text: The whispering fungus borrows a voice that once comforted someone.
r061.failure_text: Fortune fails at the crucial moment. The consequence leads to Mirror Maze.

r062.success_text: The irrigation channel smells sweet at first and metallic after the first bend.

r063.success_text: One reflection turns away before you decide to follow it.
r063.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Mirror Maze.

r064.success_text: The silver door has no lock until its reflection is touched.

r065.success_text: Your reflected hand closes around yours. When you pull back, the crescent Moon Key is cold in your palm and one reflection is missing from the maze.
r065.failure_text: The reflection grips harder than expected. You tear free injured, while the key remains on the far side of the glass.

r066.success_text: The gantry is safe only while the water remains dark.
r066.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r067.success_text: A goblin stamp matches a maintenance symbol no goblin could have understood.

r068.success_text: A red line appears across the floor like a route drawn by an invisible clerk.

r069.success_text: You remove the loose ledger page. It records that Varn's service at the Ninth Lock was never discharged—deliberately.

r070.success_text: The key opens the door backwards, into a room you thought you had already left.

r071.success_text: The bridge repeats your last words before you have spoken them.
r071.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r072.success_text: The loose stone moves only when pressed by someone who already knows it is loose.

r073.success_text: The wind sighs in relief using somebody else's voice.

r074.success_text: The beast's royal collar is older than the arena around it.
r074.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r075.success_text: The lift accepts the seal and key without noticing that both previous owners are dead.

r076.success_text: Black flame writes a doorway across a wall that had never admitted being one.

r077.success_text: The forge wakes hungry for heat, oath and something it calls authorised fuel.
r077.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Black Wind Forge.

r078.success_text: The Rune-Candle lifts from the cold anvil and burns with a black flame. Hidden words appear across the forge: AUTHORITY REQUIRES WITNESS.

r079.success_text: The hidden corridor returns you to the sound of voices falling upward.

r080.success_text: One of the nine doors opens under the word OFFICE.

r081.success_text: The moon-door opens onto stalls whose merchants have forgotten being alive.

r082.success_text: Two doors close, seven remain silent and the wall marked WITNESS opens.

r083.success_text: You name one real debt. The whispers withdraw just enough for the deeper truth to become clear: among the dead, names and memories are currency because recognised identity still carries power.

r084.success_text: Every denied promise becomes another voice at your back.
r084.failure_text: Fortune fails at the crucial moment. The consequence leads to Hall of Whispered Debts.

r085.success_text: The merchant takes one year of memory without pain. The archive route opens, and only then do you feel the clean, impossible gap in your own history.

r086.success_text: The merchant wraps Varn's ceremonial name in a strip of obituary paper. It grants leverage over the Guardian, though not release.

r087.success_text: The dead merchants do not call after customers who still own themselves.

r088.success_text: The torn ledger page fits the archive index. A hidden passage opens, and the surviving Crown law reveals that Serath is judging a successor, not merely defending treasure.

r089.success_text: The Rune-Candle restores the drowned decree. You learn both the words that can release Varn and the conditions under which Serath must recognise a claimant.

r090.success_text: Varn rises slowly, carrying nine centuries of obedience into the first blow.
r090.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r091.success_text: You name the debt, its completion and the witness. Varn's hands leave the Lock. The bronze giant's first uncommanded breath is quieter than the door opening.

r092.success_text: Varn's true name bends the Guardian to your command. The way opens, but the old obligation remains unresolved behind you.
r092.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to Guardian of the Ninth Lock.

r093.success_text: The stair exists only where you are not looking directly.
r093.failure_text: Fortune fails at the crucial moment. The consequence leads to Abyssal Stair.

r094.success_text: A drowned stair appears beneath a symbol meant for people official maps denied.

r095.success_text: The golden passage narrows whenever you imagine owning what waits beyond it.
r095.failure_text: The attempt fails before you can secure the intended route. The consequence leads to A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r096.success_text: Invisible instructions appear across the teeth: CLAIMANTS WALK BETWEEN APPETITES.

r097.success_text: Serath draws a weapon only after asking whether victory is your entire claim.
r097.failure_text: The struggle turns against you. The consequence carries you towards A Delver's Memorial Plaque.

r098.success_text: You present authority, knowledge and restraint as one coherent claim. Serath lowers the weapon by the width of a finger, then steps aside.

r099.success_text: Serath reads the unpaid debt and accepts that you understand what the custodians have endured. The Warden permits you to face the Crown.

r100.success_text: There are no coins in the vault—only a crown waiting for somebody to misunderstand it.

r101.success_text: The Crown answers your refusal with affection rather than anger.
r101.failure_text: Fortune fails at the crucial moment. The consequence leads to Cursed Possession.

r102.success_text: The old rulers fall silent only after your name survives being spoken by all of them.

r103.success_text: For a moment the Crown feels like relief.

r104.success_text: Pell turns the crossing into a sequence of knots, breaths and instructions. The bridge remains terrifying, but no longer feels arbitrary.

r107.success_text: Pell folds the contract and steps back from the Ninth Lock. He will wait above as a living witness, but he will not follow you further.

r105.success_text: Ordinary rats scatter around the little crown and refuse to touch it.

r106.success_text: The swarm's grammar is gone, but its careful inventory remains.

r108.success_text: The Registrar's key and Brass Seal override the active writ. Serath accepts the cancellation, though the Warden's expression makes clear that legality and innocence are not the same.

narrative_delivery_plan.purpose: Ensure the player receives the core adventure story even when taking shortcuts, while optional routes deepen rather than repair basic comprehension.

acts.name: Danger as Local Industry
acts.minimum_takeaway: A mythic treasure lies below the Rust Wind Hills; Barrowgate licences and profits from attempts to recover it.
acts.tone_transition: Wry frontier commerce with unease under the humour.

acts.name: The Paperwork Works
acts.minimum_takeaway: The ancient dungeon recognises seals, procedures and legal identity. Barrowgate's bureaucracy is connected to the ruins.

acts.name: What the Old State Did
acts.minimum_takeaway: The complex governed debt, identity and succession; its surviving guardians are trapped in unfinished obligations.
acts.tone_transition: Dungeon obstacles become consequences of history.

acts.name: The Treasure Is an Office
acts.minimum_takeaway: The final vault is a succession court. Taking the Crown means negotiating power, identity and inherited responsibility.
acts.tone_transition: Adventure climax becomes a personal and political choice.

continuity_rules: A shortcut may skip detail but must not skip the minimum takeaway of an act.
Knowledge should be stated when acquired, not merely added to state.
State variants should make prior choices visible at later checkpoints.
The final succession reveal must be spoken even if the archive route was bypassed.
Optional lore should deepen motives and alternatives rather than supply facts required to understand the basic scene.

notes: read_aloud is player-facing prose and is safe to present directly.
narrative.hidden_truth and similar semantic fields should not be exposed without discovery.
The current runner is expected to read node.read_aloud as a plain string.
node_presentation.sensory_emotional_guidance: Present read_aloud directly. Do not append the full sensory_focus list; it is semantic support for improvisation. Use emotional_undertone to shape delivery, not as text to expose.

notes: label remains mechanically stable and suitable for numbered choice menus.
stakes is player-facing and should not reveal hidden_truth fields.
hook provides flavour without replacing node read_aloud narration.
route_presentation.hook_style: Hooks may include one physical sensation or one small emotional tell. Avoid stacking multiple atmospheric details.

player_facing_narrative.purpose: Atmosphere, character, sensory detail and contextual exposition.

route_choice_metadata.purpose: Plain description of the available choice and its visible cost, risk or opportunity.

engine_execution.purpose: Neutral deterministic semantics used to execute the selected route.

semantic_context.purpose: Context for narration and interpretation. Hidden truths must not be exposed before discovery.

route_execution.execution_rule: Resolve the route by ID and apply its declared conditions, costs, test and effects. Do not derive mechanics from atmospheric prose.
safety_guidance: Keep route.label and route.stakes plain and mechanically descriptive.
Keep identity, ritual and metaphysical imagery in node narration or semantic context.
Do not include hidden narrative fields in the deterministic tool-call payload.
The engine_action field is descriptive metadata; route.id remains authoritative.

on_node_entry: Present the highest-priority matching node.read_aloud_variant, if any.
Present node.mandatory_exposition when present and not already contained in read_aloud.
on_route_success: Resolve mechanics using route.id and engine_action.
choice_display: Display route.label as the selectable action.
Do not require the player to infer acquired knowledge from flags.
